findings.description: The memo header fields (To, From, Subject) are rendered as a structured table in the HTML but appear as simple indented text in the PDF. While the data is preserved, the HTML introduces visible table borders/structure not present in the original.
findings.evidence: PDF shows To/From/Subject as simple label-value pairs with indentation. HTML renders these in a bordered table with distinct rows and cells.

findings.description: The NC Water Resources logo appears slightly different in cropping/rendering between PDF and HTML, though it is present and correctly positioned.
findings.evidence: PDF shows full logo with text 'Water Resources ENVIRONMENTAL QUALITY' beneath. HTML shows the same logo but with slight visual differences in rendering.

findings.description: The document uses 'MEMORANDUM' as h1, which is acceptable as a document title, but the heading hierarchy has all major section headings (I, II, III, IV) as h2 which is correct. However, the h1 is styled with underline and center alignment matching the PDF, so this is acceptable.
findings.evidence: h1: 'MEMORANDUM', h2: 'I. History/Background', 'II. March 29, 2016 Public Meeting and Oral Comments Summary', 'III. Written Public Comment Summary', 'IV. Attachments'

findings.description: The HTML has nested <main> elements, which is a structural issue but does not cause content duplication.

The memo header (To/From/Subject) is rendered as a table in the HTML whereas in the PDF it is formatted as a simple field-value layout. The table has no visible borders styled inline with 'border: none', and cell values match the PDF content accurately. This is an acceptable structural choice.
findings.evidence: PDF: 'To: Secretary Donald R. van der Vaart' etc. HTML: table with matching content.

findings.description: The second image has an empty alt text, which is generic rather than descriptive. The first image has appropriate alt text describing the NC DEQ Water Resources logo.
findings.evidence: First img: alt='North Carolina Department of Environmental Quality Water Resources logo'. Second img: alt='' (empty).
